Patch Set 4:

(5 comments)

I'm not a huge fan of the approach taken in this patch, or this section of code in general, since it can potentially double the amount of memory allocated for each of these sg arrays because it changes a 256-element array into a 257-element array just to avoid accessing the last entry.

Maybe I'm missing something critical since this isn't my area of the code, and in the end this isn't worse than what is there today so I won't give it a -1, but it feels like a workaround for bad coding rather than a proper fix.

Patch Set 4:

I understand your concern but what do we do with the last bits of data that overflow the last page? Do we just drop it and return an error?

Patch Set 4:

James, are you saying that there really _are_ 257 fragments in the RPC?  I don't understand how there can be any "last bits of data" beyond the 256th page unless the RPC is improperly formed?  That would be a bug in ptlrpc RPC creation or similar.

Patch Set 4:

I see what you are saying. So for LNet if their is a offset then the maximum size of the packet will then be 256 pages - offset to ensure that it fits in only 256 pages. If that is the case then the problem is in the nob since it shouldn't be larger than 256 pages - offset in size. This will require more investigating to find why nob is to large.

Patch Set 7:

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Doug, HPDD Checkpatch does not mark patches -1 unless they cannot be applied to master at all.  The style comments are advisory.

While I'd be happy to have them fixed if the patch is refreshed, I'm also aware that this patch is fixing a memory corruption bug, so I'd like to get it landed sooner rather than later.  The pre-existing style issues can take a back seat to getting the patch landed in this case.

parents.subject: LU-7418 tests: cancel all locks in sanity test_29

commit.subject: LU-8057 ko2iblnd: Replace sg++ with sg = sg_next(sg)
commit.message:
LU-8057 ko2iblnd: Replace sg++ with sg = sg_next(sg)

With scatterlist chaining, simply incrementing the array does not
work. sg_next macro was thus introduced to follow the chain links
when necessary. So replace sg++ with sg_next.

This change was made with the help of the following Coccinelle
semantic patch:
//<smpl>
@@
 struct scatterlist *sg;
@@
 -sg++
 +sg = sg_next(sg)
//</smpl>

In my test of the upstream client this change exposed a long
standing issues where we have a offset that is not page aligned
would cause us to access memory beyond the scatter gather list
which was causing memory corruption when all 256 fragments were
in use.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Change-Id: I5bc140645b841aa2a92da434ffa65795112c6cec
Signed-off-by: Amitoj Kaur Chawla <amitoj1606@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Greg Kroah-Hartman <gregkh@linuxfoundation.org>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/19342
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
